text.title: Project Integration Management Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Status colour declared GREEN/YELLOW/RED based on PM narrative rather than numeric threshold comparison.
antipattern.root-cause: Agents over-summarise: declaring 'GREEN — on track' without numeric backing is the dominant integration failure.
antipattern.fix: Force the threshold table into the plan; the script computes colour, the human interprets it.

antipattern.symptom: Cross-area impact analysis covers only the areas the agent has API access to.
antipattern.root-cause: An agent with only Jira access produces schedule-blind reports.
antipattern.fix: Force input audit: every knowledge area must appear in the impact memo or be explicitly marked 'data unavailable'.

antipattern.symptom: Closure declared complete with an empty lessons-learned file.
antipattern.root-cause: Agents skip lessons learned because there is no code change to be made.
antipattern.fix: Force closure checklist to fail-close if the lessons file is empty.

antipattern.symptom: Charter sign-off automated by the agent merging its own PR.
antipattern.root-cause: Charter sign-off is a governance decision requiring sponsor authority.
antipattern.fix: The agent prepares the PR and the sponsor approves the merge; CODEOWNERS gate.
